event: Rapid test diagnosis of Plasmodium falciparum (Pf) malaria
MAT titers for Leptospira ictero-haemorrhagiae of 1:400
abdominal ultrasonography showed a singleton pregnancy
referred to our institute for further management
rapid sequence intubation with cricoid pressure
anaesthesia maintained with isoflurane in N2O-O2 mixture
ultrasound guided bilateral transverse abdominis plane block
deep vein thrombosis prophylaxis (pneumatic compression) provided
computed tomography of the brain carried out
keeping blood partial pressure of carbondioxide 32-40 mmHg
chest radiograph showed bilateral non-homogeneous opacities (lower and middle zones)
bed side fibre-optic bronchoscopy carried out
blood picture indicating haemolytic uremic syndrome
significant reduction of inspired oxygen fraction (FiO2)
liver function test with coagulation parameters showed improving trends
